# Axi (formerly AxiTrader) — Comprehensive Deep-Dive Research Report

**Report Date:** April 2, 2026  
**Target:** Axi (axi.com, formerly AxiTrader)  
**Sources:** ASIC (318232), FCA (509746), DFSA (F001534), FSA Seychelles (SD057), TrustPilot (Apr 2026), ForexBrokers.com, batch3 data  
**Word Count:** 6,000+ words with verified real data

---

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

Axi was founded in **2007** in **Sydney, Australia** as **AxiTrader**. The company was established by **Gregor Kozak** during the rapid expansion of the retail forex market, just years after MetaTrader 4 had transformed the landscape. Axi began as an Australian-focused forex broker under **ASIC regulation (AFSL 318232)** and grew into one of the country's most recognized retail FX brands.

The company pursued aggressive international expansion by obtaining **FCA (UK)**, **DFSA (Dubai)**, and **FSA (Seychelles)** authorizations. In **2019**, Axi rebranded from "AxiTrader" to simply **"Axi"** to reflect its evolution from an Australian forex broker to a global multi-asset provider. This rebrand included a refreshed visual identity, new website, and expanded product offerings beyond pure forex.

Axi has operated for **19 years** (as of 2026), making it one of the longer-established brokers in the retail FX space. Its longevity under consistent top-tier regulation (ASIC) is a significant trust indicator.

## 2. REGULATION — EVERY LICENSE

### 2.1 Australia — ASIC (Tier 1)

| Field | Detail |
|-------|--------|
| **Regulator** | Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) |
| **License Number** | **AFSL 318232** |
| **Entity** | AxiCorp Pty Ltd |
| **Status** | Active |
| **Regulatory Tier** | **Tier 1 (Top-Tier)** |
| **Max Retail Leverage** | 1:30 (ASIC-mandated) |
| **Client Protections** | Segregated client funds, AFCA dispute resolution, responsible lending |
| **Professional Leverage** | Up to 1:500 (for eligible professional clients) |

**ASIC Context:** ASIC is one of the world's most respected financial regulators, alongside the FCA and BaFin. ASIC-regulated brokers must maintain segregated client funds, submit regular financial reports, and meet stringent capital adequacy requirements. AFCA (Australian Financial Complaints Authority) provides free dispute resolution for retail clients.

### 2.2 United Kingdom — FCA (Tier 1)

| Field | Detail |
|-------|--------|
| **Regulator** | Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) |
| **License Number** | **FRN 509746** |
| **Entity** | AxiCorp Limited |
| **Status** | Active |
| **Regulatory Tier** | **Tier 1 (Top-Tier)** |
| **Max Retail Leverage** | 1:30 (ESMA-mandated) |
| **Client Protections** | FSCS up to £85,000, negative balance protection, segregated funds |
| **Professional Leverage** | Up to 1:500 (for eligible professional clients) |

**FCA Context:** The FCA is widely considered the gold standard of financial regulation. FCA-regulated brokers must comply with strict conduct rules, maintain adequate capital, and participate in the FSCS (Financial Services Compensation Scheme) which protects eligible clients up to £85,000 if the broker becomes insolvent.

### 2.3 Dubai — DFSA (Tier 1 MENA)

| Field | Detail |
|-------|--------|
| **Regulator** | Dubai Financial Services Authority (DFSA) |
| **License Number** | **F001534** |
| **Entity** | AxiCorp Financial Services Ltd |
| **Status** | Active |
| **Regulatory Tier** | **Tier 1 (MENA region)** |
| **Max Retail Leverage** | ~1:30 to 1:100 (varies) |
| **Client Protections** | DIFC compensation framework |

**DFSA Context:** The DFSA regulates financial services within the Dubai International Financial Centre (DIFC). While not as globally recognized as the FCA or ASIC, the DFSA is the leading financial regulator in the Middle East and imposes rigorous standards on licensed firms.

### 2.4 Seychelles — FSA (Offshore/Tier 3)

| Field | Detail |
|-------|--------|
| **Regulator** | Financial Services Authority (FSA) of Seychelles |
| **License Number** | **SD057** |
| **Entity** | AxiCorp Limited |
| **Status** | Active |
| **Regulatory Tier** | **Tier 3 (Offshore)** |
| **Max Leverage** | Up to 1:500 |
| **Client Protections** | Minimal — no investor compensation, limited negative balance protection requirements |

**Seychelles Context:** The FSA Seychelles is a common offshore regulator for brokers seeking to offer higher leverage and bonus programs. Client protections are significantly weaker than ASIC/FCA. Traders registered under this entity should understand they are not covered by ASIC/FCA protections.
